scores.candidate_floor: loop body solved in pure C (pins retired); 3-store epilogue UNSOLVED legitimately

category: INCOMPLETE — rejected match (cheat epilogue); sound loop-fix preserved

sessions.lever: loop body: offset-indexed source reads *(s32*)((u8*)arg0+ofs) + split-init i12 -> retired 4 register-asm pins (pure C). Epilogue zero-stores REJECTED.
sessions.lever_rule: walking-pointer/split-init (loop, sound); dual-spelling addressing steer (epilogue, FORBIDDEN)
sessions.git_ref_cheat_form: dfb9e9ac on work/orch3a (do NOT reuse the epilogue)

reviewer.evidence: Epilogue writes p[0] two ways (p-indexed AND the full *(s32*)((u8*)&D_800F1198+i12) expression) to force two different MIPS addressing modes for the same lvalue. No semantic purpose -> codegen steer; fails 6-test 1/2/3.

remaining_gap.summary: Loop body sound + pins retired. Only the 3 trailing zero-stores to &D_800F1198+i12{,+4,+8} are unsolved: target mixes a %hi/%lo-split store (+0) with full-address stores (+4/+8) - a GCC addressing-mode artifact the rejected cheat forced via dual-spelling.
remaining_gap.needs: a CONSISTENT pure-C epilogue (no same-lvalue-two-spellings) that GCC compiles to the target's addressing-mode mix; else document as a genuine plateau.

next_hypotheses: Keep dfb9e9ac's loop-body fix; replace ONLY the epilogue with a consistent form; re-measure honest distance.
Zero the 3 words via the array base symbols (D_800F1198/9C/A0 + i12) in index order to see GCC's natural addressing.
If no consistent C reaches the split/full mix, plateau (NOT a cheat-epilogue).
rejected_forms: epilogue dual-spelling: p[0] written as *(s32*)((u8*)&D_800F1198+i12)=0 alongside p[1]/p[2] - codegen steer, FAILed retro-audit
